# Year over Year (YoY)

Step-by-Step Guide to Understanding Year over Year (YoY) Growth

Updated Apr. 7, 2025 

4m Read Time

 Part of the Guide On... 

 Market Research  Part of the Guide On: 

 Market Research 

 Market Research Fundamental Concepts 

[SWOT Analysis](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/swot-analysis/)[Porter’s 5 Forces Model](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/porters-five-forces-model/)[BCG Growth Share Matrix](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/bcg-matrix/)[Tailwinds](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/tailwinds/)

 Growth Metrics 

[Growth Rate](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/growth-rate/)Year over Year (YoY)[Month over Month Growth (M/M)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/month-over-month-growth-rate/)[Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/cagr-compound-annual-growth-rate/)[Compound Monthly Growth Rate (CMGR)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/compound-monthly-growth-rate-cmgr/)[Average Annual Growth Rate (AAGR)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/average-annual-growth-rate-aagr/)[Sustainable Growth Rate (SGR)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/sustainable-growth-rate/)[Internal Growth Rate (IGR)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/internal-growth-rate/)[Inorganic Growth](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/inorganic-growth/)[Organic Growth](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/organic-growth/)[Reinvestment Rate](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/reinvestment-rate/)

[Economies of Scale](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/economies-of-scale/)[Diseconomies of Scale](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/diseconomies-of-scale/)[Switching Costs](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/switching-costs/)[Barriers to Entry](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/barriers-to-entry/)[Network Effects](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/network-effects/)[Pricing Power](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/pricing-power/)[Headwinds](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/headwinds/)[Revenue Streams](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/revenue-streams/)[Resale Value](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/resale-value/)[Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/oem-original-equipment-manufacturer/)

1.   [What is YoY?](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/year-over-year-yoy/#h2_1)
2.   [How to Calculate YoY Growth](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/year-over-year-yoy/#h2_2)

9.   [2. YoY Growth Calculation in Excel](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/year-over-year-yoy/#h2_9)
10.   [3. YoY Growth Analysis Example](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/year-over-year-yoy/#h2_10)

## What is YoY?

The **Year over Year (YoY)**growth is the percentage change in an annualized metric over two comparable periods, most often the current and prior period.

By comparing a company’s current annual financial performance to that of 12 months back, the rate at which the company has grown as well as any cyclical patterns can be identified.

![Image 8: Year over Year (YoY)](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/uploads/2022/01/18155326/Year-over-Year-Growth-Formula-960x300.jpg)

Generating

Generate Key Takeaways

*   YoY stands for “year-over-year” and measures the rate of change in a metric across two comparable periods.
*   The YoY growth rate can be used to analyze the annual increase or decrease in a financial metric, such as revenue.
*   The YoY metric is most often used to determine the growth rate over the prior period, i.e. the rate of growth at which the financial metric grow by to reach the ending period value.
*   The formula to calculate the YoY growth rate is to divide the current period balance by the beginning period balance, and then subtracting by one.

## How to Calculate YoY Growth

YoY stands for “Year-over-Year”, and measures the rate of [growth](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/growth-rate/) in a specific metric over two comparable periods, such as the current and prior period.

The objective of performing a year over year growth analysis (YoY) is to compare recent financial performance to historical periods.

The question answered by performing a YoY growth analysis is, _“Has the business been growing at a faster pace than the previous year, or has growth been slowing down in recent years?”_

To calculate the YoY [growth rate](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/growth-rate/), the current period amount is divided by the prior period amount, and then one is subtracted to get to a percentage rate.

## YoY Formula

The formula used to calculate the year over year (YoY) growth divides the current period value by the prior period value, and then subtracts by one.

**Year-over-Year Growth (YoY) =** (Current Period Value **÷** Prior Period Value) **–** 1

Where:

*   Current Period → End of Period (EoP)
*   Prior Period → Beginning of Period (BoP)

## YoY Growth Calculation Example

For example, suppose the net [operating income](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/operating-income/) (NOI) of a commercial real estate property investment has grown from $25 million in Year 0 to $30 million in Year 1.

*   Beginning NOI (Year 0) = $25 million
*   Ending NOI (Year 1) = $30 million
*   Increase / (Decrease) = $30 million – $25 million = $5 million

After inputting our assumptions into the formula, we arrive at an YoY growth rate of 20% in the [net operating income (NOI)](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/noi-net-operating-income/) of the property.

*   NOI YoY Growth (%) = ($30 million ÷ $25 million) – 1 = 20.0%

Alternatively, another method to calculate the YoY growth is to subtract the prior period balance from the current period balance, and then divide that amount by the prior period balance.

*   NOI YoY Growth (%) = ($30 million – $25 million) ÷ $25 million = 20.0%

Under either approach, the year over year (YoY) growth rate in the property’s NOI is 20.0%, which reflects the [percentage change](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/percent-change/) between the two periods.

## What is a Good YoY Growth Rate?

The main benefit of YoY growth analysis is how easy it is to track and compare growth rates across several periods. If the growth metric is annualized, the adjustment removes the impact of monthly [volatility](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/volatility/).

Furthermore, cyclical patterns become apparent if the analysis with historical results is inclusive of a minimum of one full economic cycle.

While the intuition is easy to grasp for the two basic rules, you must perform deeper diligence into the company’s growth trajectory to identify the core underlying drivers behind the change, before reaching a definitive conclusion.

*   Increase in YoY Growth Rate → Positive Outlook in Growth Trajectory
*   Decrease in YoY Growth Rate → Negative Outlook in Growth Trajectory

Briefly, consider a company whose [revenue](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/net-revenue/) growth rate in the past year was 5%, but whose growth rate was merely 3% in the current year.

However, the quality of the revenue generated could have improved despite the slightly lower growth rate (e.g. longer-term contractual revenue, less [churn](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/churn-rate/), fewer customer acquisition costs).

On that note, it would be inaccurate to assume that the current year was necessarily “worse” than the prior year without a deeper dive analysis.

In addition, another important consideration is that growth inevitably slows down eventually for all companies.

Late-stage, mature companies with established market shares are less likely to allocate funds to facilitate more growth (e.g. reinvestment, [capital expenditures](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/capital-expenditure-capex/)).

Instead, such companies are more inclined to focus on the following types of corporate actions:

*   Issue Dividends to Common Shareholders (or Preferred Shareholders)
*   Integrate Changes to Improve Operating Efficiency (i.e. Higher Profit Margins, Free Cash Flow Conversion)
*   Extract More Value from Existing Customer Base (Upselling / Cross-Selling)
*   Increased Spending on New Customer Acquisition Strategies
*   Improve Customer Retention Rate (or Reduce Customer Churn Rate)

![Image 9: AI in Finance](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/wspimage/wsp_ai_finance.svg)
## **Use AI for Deeper YoY Analysis Insights**

Year-over-Year (YoY) analysis is a foundational tool in financial reporting, enabling professionals to track growth, seasonality, and operational efficiency across comparable time periods. As organizations grapple with increasingly complex datasets, artificial intelligence is proving essential in enhancing YoY analysis by streamlining data aggregation, surfacing emerging patterns, and generating predictive insights. These innovations are especially relevant in today’s financial landscape, which is why Wall Street Prep, in collaboration with Columbia Business School Executive Education, developed the [AI for Business & Finance Certificate Program](https://wallstreetprep.business.columbia.edu/ai-certification/). By exploring how technologies like AI are reshaping [growth trends](https://wallstreetprep.business.columbia.edu/ai-certification/), this program helps professionals rethink how they approach [year-over-year performance](https://wallstreetprep.business.columbia.edu/ai-certification/).

[Learn the AI for Finance Skill Set](https://wallstreetprep.business.columbia.edu/ai-certification/?utm_source=wsp&utm_campaign=wspSiteRefer&utm_content=inlineTextAd)

## YoY Growth Calculator — Excel Template

We’ll now move on to a modeling exercise, which you can access by filling out the form below.

First Name* 

Last Name* 

Email* 

WSP_last_form_interest 

WSP_last_form_completed_action 

WSP_last_form_completed_page_name 

WSP_last_form_completed_page_url 

WSP_last_form_completed_datetime 

WSP_last_form_completed_file_url 

WSP_last_form_completed_file_name 

wsp_last_form_ft_utm_campaign 

wsp_last_form_ft_utm_content 

wsp_last_form_ft_utm_medium 

wsp_last_form_ft_utm_source 

wsp_last_form_lt_utm_content 

wsp_last_form_lt_utm_campaign 

wsp_last_form_lt_utm_medium 

wsp_last_form_lt_utm_source 

## 1. Revenue Growth Rate Assumptions

Suppose we’re analyzing the growth profile of a company that generated $100 million in revenue and $25 million in operating income ([EBIT](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/ebit/)) in the [trailing twelve months](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/ttm-trailing-twelve-months/).

Selected Financial Data (Year 0)

1.   Revenue (or “Sales”), Year 0 = $100 million
2.   Operating Income (or “EBIT”), Year 0 = $25 million

Our first step is to project the company’s revenue and operating income (EBIT) using the following assumptions.

*   Revenue YoY Growth Rate = +4%
*   Operating Income (EBIT) YoY Growth Rate = -3%

## 2. YoY Growth Calculation in Excel

If we multiply the prior period balance by (1 + growth rate assumption), we can calculate the projected current period balance.

For example, in Year 0 (12/31/21), the revenue is $100m, so the next period’s revenue is $104m after applying the 4.0% YoY growth assumption.

*   Revenue, Year 1 = $100 million × (1 + 4.0%) = $104 million

Once we perform the same process for revenue in all forecasted periods, as well as for EBIT, the next part of our modeling exercise is to calculate the YoY growth rate.

[![Image 10: YoY Growth Calculation Example in Excel](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/uploads/2021/11/02050206/YoY-Growth-Calculation-Example-in-Excel.jpg)](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/uploads/2021/11/02050206/YoY-Growth-Calculation-Example-in-Excel.jpg)

## 3. YoY Growth Analysis Example

Here, by dividing the current period amount by the prior period amount, and then subtracting 1, we arrive at the implied growth rate.

In Year 1, we divide $104m by $100m and subtract one to get 4.0%, which reflects the growth rate from the preceding year.

The YoY growth of our company can be analyzed for an improved understanding of its growth trajectory, the implied stage of the company’s life-cycle, and cyclical trends in [operating performance](https://www.wallstreetprep.com/knowledge/ebitda/).

[![Image 11: YoY Growth Calculator](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/uploads/2021/11/04105207/YoY-Growth-Calculator.jpg)](https://media.wallstreetprep.com/uploads/2021/11/04105207/YoY-Growth-Calculator.jpg)
